title: JailPopulationMetric
description: An aggregate criminal-justice-system indicator MOCJ analyzes and reports — jail population (daily census, admissions, discharges), arraignments, arrests, or re-arrest rates. AGGREGATE ONLY; never an individual. MOCJ does NOT own or publish these numbers as data: it produces PDF 'explainers' (WordPress data_reports / data_stories) and links out to the source agencies (DOC Daily Population in Custody, NYPD CompStat, NYS DCJS, Board of Correction). This schema is a proposed machine-readable shape for the metrics that today exist only inside those explainers and other agencies' dashboards.

metric.description: Which system indicator this row measures.

unit.description: Unit of the value (persons, admissions, percent).

sourceAgency.description: The agency that actually owns the number — MOCJ convenes and analyzes but rarely owns it.

sourceReport.description: The MOCJ explainer / data report this metric was drawn from, if any.
